name: rén
mnemonic: A pictograph of a person walking — two legs striding forward. As a left-side radical it contracts to 亻 (a slimmer standing figure).
signals: Most characters with 亻 relate to people: their roles, actions, or kinds of person.

name: nǚ
mnemonic: A pictograph of a woman kneeling with her arms gracefully crossed.
signals: Characters with 女 relate to women, family members, gentleness, and (in old usage) certain social qualities.

name: zǐ
mnemonic: A pictograph of a baby in swaddling clothes — arms waving out, legs wrapped.
signals: Indicates children, descendants, seeds, or small things. Also a frequent diminutive suffix in nouns like 桌子.

name: xīn
mnemonic: A pictograph of the heart's chambers and blood vessels. As a left-side radical it becomes 忄 (a vertical heart).
signals: Found in emotion and thought words — anything involving feeling, thinking, or attitude.

name: shǒu
mnemonic: A pictograph of a hand with five fingers. As a left-side radical it becomes 扌 — a vertical hand reaching.
signals: Almost always means an action done with the hand: hitting, grabbing, pushing, holding.

name: kǒu
mnemonic: A square — an open mouth (also doorway/opening). One of the most common radicals.
signals: Found in characters about speaking, eating, sounds, particles, and openings.

name: mù
mnemonic: A rectangle with two horizontal strokes inside — iris and pupil of an eye, drawn vertically.
signals: Found in characters about seeing, watching, sleeping (closed eyes), and observation.

name: ěr
mnemonic: A pictograph of the ear with its outer ridges.
signals: Found in characters about hearing, taking (ancient warriors took ears as trophies), and quietness.

name: yuè / ròu
mnemonic: Two meanings collapsed into one shape! 月 'moon' looks like a crescent. 肉 'flesh' simplified to the same 月 form when used as a left-side radical.
signals: Right-side 月 = moon/time (明, 朋, 期). Left-side 月 = body parts/flesh (脸, 脚, 腿, 肚子, 背). Context tells you which.

name: zú
mnemonic: Top is 口 (a body) over 止 (a foot/stop). The leg-and-foot combined.
signals: Indicates actions of the foot: walking, running, kicking, stepping.

name: rì
mnemonic: A rectangle with a line across the middle — the sun disk with its features.
signals: Found in characters about the sun, brightness, time of day, and dates.

name: shuǐ
mnemonic: A central stream with splashes on both sides. As a left-side radical it becomes 氵 — three falling drops.
signals: EXTREMELY common. Almost always means liquid: water, drink, river, ocean, washing, alcohol.

name: huǒ
mnemonic: A flame with sparks on either side. At the bottom of characters it becomes 灬 — four dots representing embers/smoke.
signals: Found in characters about fire, heat, cooking, and (with 灬) things involving fire or animal-roasting.

name: tǔ
mnemonic: A plant or cross marking horizontal ground — the foundation of the earth.
signals: Found in characters about soil, ground, places, and earthen things (cities, blocks).

name: mù
mnemonic: A central trunk with branches reaching up and roots going down.
signals: Found in characters about trees, wooden items, and furniture. Doubled is 林 (woods), tripled is 森 (forest).

name: shān
mnemonic: Three peaks rising from the horizon — the central one tallest.
signals: Found in characters about mountains, height, peaks, and elevation.

name: yǔ
mnemonic: A flat sky/cloud with raindrops falling through underneath.
signals: Found in weather characters — rain, snow, thunder, dew, fog.

name: tián
mnemonic: A square plot divided into four sections by paths between crops.
signals: Indicates fields, farming, grids, and (because the brain was once drawn like a field) thinking.

name: shí
mnemonic: 厂 (cliff) over 口 (a rock) — a stone fallen from a cliff.
signals: Indicates stones, minerals, and hard things.

name: bīng
mnemonic: Two ice crystals stacked. Distinguish from 氵 (water): 冫 has only two drops, 氵 has three.
signals: Found in cold-temperature characters.

name: cǎo
mnemonic: Two stalks of grass sprouting upward — the 'grass head' that sits on top of characters.
signals: VERY common. Nearly always means a plant, herb, flower, or vegetable.

name: zhú
mnemonic: Two stalks of bamboo with hanging leaves. The 'bamboo head'.
signals: Indicates things made of bamboo: writing implements (pens were brushes), instruments, baskets, counting.

name: mǐ
mnemonic: A single grain of rice with its husk strands sticking out in four directions.
signals: Found in characters about grains, powder, and rice-based foods.

name: hé
mnemonic: A grain stalk drooping over from a heavy head of grain at the top — picture wheat or rice ready for harvest.
signals: Indicates cereal crops and harvest-related concepts (autumn, peace through full bellies).

name: quǎn
mnemonic: A dog on its hind legs. As a left-side radical it becomes 犭 — the 'beast' radical.
signals: Found in characters about dogs and other four-legged animals.

name: niǎo
mnemonic: A pictograph of a bird in profile — head with eye (the dot), body, tail.
signals: Found in names of birds.

name: chóng
mnemonic: A pictograph of a coiled worm or insect.
signals: Found in characters about insects, reptiles, sea creatures (anything cold-blooded or creepy-crawly).

name: niú
mnemonic: A cow's head seen straight-on: horns and ears at the top, face below.
signals: Found in characters about cattle and (because oxen were key to property) sometimes things/objects.

name: yáng
mnemonic: A sheep's face from the front: two curved horns on top, then the face.
signals: Sheep symbolized good things in ancient China. Found in characters about sheep and goodness/beauty.

name: mǎ
mnemonic: A simplified horse: mane on top, body, four legs (now one sweep) at the bottom.
signals: Found in characters about horses, and as a phonetic in many words (because 'mǎ' is a common sound).

name: yán
mnemonic: Originally 言 — a mouth with sound waves coming out. As a left-side radical it contracts to 讠.
signals: VERY common. Almost always means an act involving speech or written words.

name: jiàn
mnemonic: Top is 目 (eye), bottom is 儿 (legs) — an eye walking around to see.
signals: Found in characters about seeing and visual recognition.

name: lì
mnemonic: A pictograph of a powerful flexed arm.
signals: Indicates force, effort, ability, and exertion.

name: yòu
mnemonic: A pictograph of a right hand with three fingers showing.
signals: An older 'hand' radical (different from 扌). Appears in characters about grasping, hand-actions, and repetition.

name: dāo
mnemonic: 刀 is a knife with a handle. On the right side of characters it becomes 刂 — a vertical blade.
signals: Found in characters about cutting, separating, and decisive actions.

name: pū
mnemonic: A hand holding a stick — the 'rap with a stick' radical, often used for forceful action.
signals: Found in characters about correction, discipline, action with force, and 'making something happen'.

name: jīn
mnemonic: 金 is a roof over 王 (precious things). As a left-side radical it becomes 钅 — a slim 'metal' marker.
signals: Indicates metals, metal tools, coins, and modern technology made of metal.

name: bèi
mnemonic: A stylized cowrie shell — the earliest form of currency in China.
signals: Found in characters about money, wealth, value, buying, and selling.

name: sī
mnemonic: Twisted strands of silk thread — a coiled bundle.
signals: Indicates silk, thread, fabric, and (by extension) connection, lineage, and continuity.

name: yī
mnemonic: 衣 is a robe with sleeves. As a left-side radical it becomes 衤. Distinguish from 礻 (altar): 衤 has TWO dots at the bottom of the right side, 礻 has ONE.
signals: Found in characters about clothing and fabric.

name: shí
mnemonic: 食 is a lidded jar of food. As a left-side radical it becomes 饣 — a vertical food container.
signals: Found in characters about eating, food preparation, and hunger/fullness.

name: yù
mnemonic: 玉 has a dot showing it's distinct jade; 王 (no dot) means 'king'. As a radical, they share the same form 王.
signals: Found in characters about jade, gems, and (since jade represented royalty) precious round objects like balls and beads.

name: bǎo
mnemonic: A roof with a vertical post — sometimes called the 'roof radical' or 'baby roof' (because of its small house shape).
signals: Found in characters about buildings, family, and being indoors/safe.

name: hǎn
mnemonic: A cliff face — a horizontal stroke with a vertical face dropping down.
signals: Found in characters about cliffs, factories, and structures built against a wall.

name: mén
mnemonic: A swinging double-leaf gate viewed from the front. The single-leaf version 户 is a different radical.
signals: Indicates doors, gates, and being inside/between.

name: hù
mnemonic: Half of a 门 — a single-leaf door, like the door of a hut.
signals: Found in characters about doors, households, and a few related concepts (writing brush, protection).

name: fù / yì
mnemonic: TWO different radicals collapsed to the same shape! 阝 on the LEFT side = 阜 (hill/mound). 阝 on the RIGHT side = 邑 (city/region).
signals: Left 阝 = terrain/elevation (hills, descent, shade). Right 阝 = places/regions/cities.

name: chuò
mnemonic: Three dots above + a foot/path stroke — a foot moving along a road. Always wraps around the bottom-left of a character.
signals: EXTREMELY common. Indicates movement, going somewhere, walking, or paths.

name: nè
mnemonic: A person lying on a bed propped up under a roof — the 'sickness' radical.
signals: Found exclusively in characters about illness, pain, and disease.

name: shì
mnemonic: 示 is an altar with offerings hanging from it. As a left-side radical it becomes 礻 — looks like 衤(clothing) but with only ONE dot on the bottom right.
signals: Found in characters about gods, spirits, religion, ritual, and blessings.

name: zǒu
mnemonic: Top is a person leaning forward, bottom is 止 (a foot) — striding forward at speed.
signals: Distinct from 辶 — 走 indicates more deliberate or fast motion (running, getting up).

name: chē
mnemonic: A top-down pictograph of a chariot: two wheels (horizontal lines), an axle, and a yoke.
signals: Found in characters about vehicles, transport, and (since chariots were heavy) weight/lightness.
